summary: German author of 'rubble literature' (Trümmerliteratur). Nobel laureate (1972). Member of Group 47. Known for Billiards at Half-Past Nine, The Lost Honour of Katharina Blum, and the short story 'Stranger, Bear Word to the Spartans We...'

works.name: Billiards at Half-Past Nine
works.description: Novel chronicling three generations of the Faehmel family of architects.

examples: chronicles three generations of the Faehmel family of architects and their work at St. Anthony's Abbey

works.description: Novella about a housekeeper who shoots a tabloid journalist.

works.name: 'Stranger, Bear Word to the Spartans We...'
works.description: Short story about a wounded soldier who realizes his hospital is his old school.

clues.clue: Wounded soldier realizes the hospital is his old high school after seeing the title truncated phrase on a blackboard; lost three limbs
examples: a wounded soldier realizes he's in his old high school after seeing the phrase 'Stranger, Bear Word to the Spartans We...' on a blackboard

clues.clue: Ends as protagonist whispers 'Milk...' to a fireman/janitor Mr. Birgeler

clues.clue: Member of Group 47 (along with Günter Grass); won 'Black Sheep' prize
examples: Boll, with other German authors such as Gunter Grass, was a member of this influential literary group
What German author of 'rubble literature' wrote about the Faehmel family
This other German writer won the Nobel Prize for Literature in 1972

comprehensive_summary:
Heinrich Böll (1917-1985) was the foremost German author of 'rubble literature' (Trümmerliteratur), the sparse postwar German literary movement depicting the destruction of Germany. He was a member of Group 47 and won the Nobel Prize in 1972.

Billiards at Half-Past Nine is set on a single day and chronicles three generations of the Faehmel family of architects. Robert Faehmel designs St. Anthony's Abbey and later orders its destruction. He plays billiards for 90 minutes daily at the Prince Heinrich Hotel. The novel uses the motif of beast vs. lamb. Johanna attempts to shoot a government minister, and Hugo is adopted.

The Lost Honour of Katharina Blum follows a housekeeper whose life is ruined by tabloid journalism after she sleeps with a bank robber. She shoots the reporter Werner Tötges after he asks her for sex. Böll was himself attacked by the tabloid Bild-Zeitung.

The short story 'Stranger, Bear Word to the Spartans We...' features a wounded soldier who realizes his hospital is his old school after seeing the truncated Spartan phrase on a blackboard. He has lost three limbs and whispers 'Milk...' to a janitor-turned-fireman.
